cpu_breakdown._NOTE: havok/ai/render_submit are CALL-SITE redirects: each *_hook_ids gives an Address Library id + the byte offset of the CALL instruction to patch (like papyrus.vm_hook's 0x7F). Safe to leave true: the plugin validates every id against your runtime's Address Library WITHOUT crashing on a miss, and refuses to patch unless the resolved address is a real CALL (0xE8). render_submit ships VERIFIED defaults (75461 SE / 77246 AE @ 0x9, from OpenAnimationReplacer's present hook) -- note it brackets Present, so its time includes vsync/GPU wait. havok and ai have NO verified id (0 = self-skip, logged); supply id_se/id_ae/offset to activate. id 0 means 'use the plugin default'. See docs/address-library.md.

papyrus._NOTE: vm_hook patches the AttemptFunctionCall call site inside VirtualMachine::ProcessMessageQueue (offset 0x7F). Defaults id_se=98130 / id_ae=104853 are taken from DennisSoemers/PapyrusProfiler and verified for SE 1.5.97 and AE 1.6.640+. Override id_se / id_ae below to retarget for a different runtime; 0 means use the compiled-in default.
